formats.format: $code$ $unix.time$ $device.hostname$ $event.type$ src=$src.ip.address$ dst=$dst.ip.address$ protocol=$meta.event.name$ sport=$src.port.number$ dport=$dst.port.number$ pattern: $pattern{regex=(\w+)\s(\w+)}$

$code$ $unix.time$ $device.hostname$ $event.type$ src=$src.ip.address$ dst=$dst.ip.address$ protocol=$meta.event.name$ sport=$src.port.number$ dport=$dst.port.number$ translated_src_ip=$translated_src.ip$ translated_port=$translated_dst.port$

$code$ $unix.time$ $device.hostname$ $event.type$ $event.network.connectionStatus$ src=$src.ip.address$ dst=$dst.ip.address$ mac=$src_endpoint.mac$ protocol=$meta.event.name$ sport=$src.port.number$ dport=$dst.port.number$

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for cisco logs migrated from samples-main
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of cisco logs in appropriate format
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

analysis.semantic_summary: Parser processes Cisco Meraki firewall and access point logs, handling VPN firewall events, IP flows, and network connection flows. It standardizes network security events by mapping source/destination IPs, ports, protocols and connection statuses to normalized fields for security monitoring and analysis.

data_source_analysis.product: Meraki MX Firewall and Access Points

optimization_opportunities: Consolidate duplicate rewrite rules across formats
Simplify regex patterns for better performance
Add support for additional Meraki log types
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: cisco_meraki_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Network Activity (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:03:13.869028

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local IP_PATTERN = "^%d+%.%d+%.%d+%.%d+$"
local PORT_PATTERN = "^%d+$"

-- Cached string operations
local format = string.format
local match = string.match

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with local references
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "Network Activity", 
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "Network & Systems Activity",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            version = "1.0.0",
            product = {
                vendor_name = "Cisco",
                name = "Meraki MX Firewall"
            }
        },
        src_endpoint = {},
        dst_endpoint = {},
        connection_info = {}
    }

    -- Optimized field transformations using local vars
    local src_ip = record["src.ip.address"]
    local dst_ip = record["dst.ip.address"]
    local src_port = record["src.port.number"]
    local dst_port = record["dst.port.number"]
    local protocol = record["meta.event.name"]
    local event_type = record["event.type"]

    -- IP Address validation and transformation
    if src_ip and match(src_ip, IP_PATTERN) then
        output.src_endpoint.ip = src_ip
    end

    if dst_ip and match(dst_ip, IP_PATTERN) then
        output.dst_endpoint.ip = dst_ip
    end

    -- Port validation and transformation 
    if src_port and match(src_port, PORT_PATTERN) then
        output.src_endpoint.port = tonumber(src_port)
    end

    if dst_port and match(dst_port, PORT_PATTERN) then
        output.dst_endpoint.port = tonumber(dst_port)
    end

    -- Protocol and activity name mapping
    if protocol then
        output.connection_info.protocol_name = protocol
    end

    if event_type then
        output.activity_name = event_type
    end

    -- Add device context if available
    if record["device.hostname"] then
        output.observables = {
            hostname = record["device.hostname"]
        }
    end

    -- Timestamp handling
    local unix_time = record["unix.time"]
    if unix_time then
        output.time = tonumber(unix_time) * 1000 -- Convert to milliseconds
    else
        output.time = os.time() * 1000
    end

    -- Validation of required OCSF fields
    if not output.src_endpoint.ip and not output.dst_endpoint.ip then
        return nil, "Missing required IP address fields"
    end

    if not output.activity_name then
        output.activity_name = "network_flow"
    end

    -- Add severity if available
    if record.code then
        output.severity = tonumber(record.code) or 0
    end

    -- Status mapping
    if record["event.network.connectionStatus"] then
        output.status = record["event.network.connectionStatus"]
    end

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end
